_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-4 CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-4: CHEMPACK / MEDDRUN — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Establish procedures for activation and use of Michigan's pre-positioned nerve agent / organophosphate antidote and pharmaceutical caches (CHEMPACK and MEDDRUN) and define EMS, Communications Agency, storage site, transportation agency, and BEPESOC responsibilities.

sections.title: Program Overview
content: CHEMPACK: CDC Strategic National Stockpile program — pre-positioned nerve agent antidote kits (Duodote / Mark I) at designated locations throughout Michigan
MEDDRUN: Michigan Emergency Drug Delivery and Resource Utilization Network — standardized caches of medications and supplies strategically located throughout Michigan
Purpose: rapidly deliver medications and medical supplies when local supplies are not adequate or become exhausted; MEDDRUN goal is MedPack deployment within 15 minutes of request
CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN may support nerve agent, biological, chemical, radiological, dirty bomb, or mass casualty incidents

BEPESOC: Bureau of Emergency Preparedness, EMS, and Systems of Care — provides follow-up, authentication, confirmation/recall coordination, MSP notification, MI-HAN alert coordination, and formulary distribution coordination

sections.title: Contact Numbers
content: PRIMARY CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN Contact: 877-633-7786 (24/7)
BACKUP Contact: 616-391-5330 (24/7)
Only authorized agencies and officials can request MEDDRUN; EMS identifies need and reports through Central Dispatch or hospital/MCA

sections.title: Activation Criteria
content: Recognition of need may come from EMS, hospital, public health, EOC, or emergency management
Biological, chemical, radiological, dirty bomb, or mass casualty incident where CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN supplies may be needed
Need for nerve agent antidote support or dirty bomb supplies
Existing local supplies are depleting or exhausted with multiple lives remaining at risk
MEDDRUN may also be pre-deployed for special events designated by state and regional leadership

sections.title: EMS Responsibilities
content: 1. EMS identifies need for medication support
2. EMS contacts Central Dispatch or a hospital/MCA
3. Central Dispatch or hospital/MCA contacts the MEDDRUN and/or CHEMPACK Communications Agency
4. Provide Essential Elements of Information (EEI) report through the requesting pathway
5. Confer with Incident Commander and keep IC informed of activation/deployment status
6. Incident Command receives arriving cache and completes the Transfer of Custody form
7. IC ensures accurate antidote accounting with the senior medical/EMT at the scene
8. If additional antidotes are required, IC informs Central Dispatch/911
9. If fewer antidotes are needed than anticipated, transport vehicle remains nearby to return unused antidotes to CSS POC

sections.title: Essential Elements of Information (EEI) Report
content: When requesting deployment, provide:
1. Name, position/title, and contact information for the individual requesting deployment
2. Name, position/title, employer, and contact information for the physician/officer in charge of medical management at the scene if different
3. Incident location: jurisdiction name, closest intersection, or site name
4. Estimated number of casualties
5. Symptoms of casualties, including pinpoint pupils, twitching, dimness of vision, seizures, slurred speech, chest tightness, difficulty breathing, or unconsciousness
6. Whether local supplies of antidotes/pharmaceuticals are exhausted, multiple lives remain at risk, and CHEMPACK supplies are needed to save lives

sections.title: BEPESOC Responsibilities
content: Contact the requesting agency to authenticate the request
Contact Communications Agency to provide confirmation or initiate recall; advise whether Alert Orders should be initiated
Contact Michigan State Police East Lansing Operations Center
Coordinate potential inter-hospital formulary distribution
Coordinate a MI-HAN Alert
Review preliminary After Action Reports with intent of improving future responses
Coordinate restocking with the Regional Emergency Preparedness Coordinator depending on availability of federal funds

sections.title: Restocking and Accountability
content: Within 72 hours of deployment, Agencies, BEPESOC, and Communications prepare a preliminary After Action Report using BEPESOC format
Restocking and return to service should occur as quickly as possible
The agency may be returned to limited service with a partially depleted MedPack/CHEMPACK
Regional Emergency Preparedness Coordinator, in collaboration with BEPESOC, is responsible for ordering supplies to restock used MedPack/CHEMPACK resources depending on federal fund availability
BEPESOC and Communications must be notified when the MedPack/CHEMPACK is returned to full service

sections.title: Deployment Algorithm
content: STEP 1: Need for nerve agent antidote support, dirty bomb supplies, or other MEDDRUN/CHEMPACK support is identified
STEP 2: Confer with Incident Commander
STEP 3: Provide report to Central Dispatch or hospital/MCA
STEP 4: Central Dispatch or requesting agency contacts MEDDRUN/CHEMPACK Communications Agency
STEP 5: Communications Agency issues deployment orders to selected MEDDRUN Dispatch and/or CHEMPACK POC/APOC
STEP 6: BEPESOC POC authenticates request and provides confirmation or recall through Communications Agency
STEP 7: Selected storage site moves desired cache to loading area and the designated transportation agency delivers supplies
STEP 8: Incident Command receives cache, completes Transfer of Custody, and maintains accurate accounting

key_drugs: Duodote Auto-Injectors — atropine 2.1mg + pralidoxime 600mg (primary CHEMPACK kit)
Mark I Auto-Injectors — atropine 2mg + pralidoxime 600mg (older format)
Diazepam auto-injectors — seizure treatment (CHEMPACK cache supplement)
IV Atropine / IV Pralidoxime — for sustained antidote infusion (cache supplement)
deterioration_flags: Local antidote supply exhausted — activate CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN IMMEDIATELY; do NOT delay
Coordinator unreachable on primary number — use backup: 616-391-5330
Incident escalation (additional casualties) — update EEI report; request additional cache deployment
scope_notes: CHEMPACK/MEDDRUN activation is routed through Central Dispatch, hospital/MCA, or other authorized agency pathways to the Communications Agency; EMS personnel who identify need should report through those pathways rather than bypassing them. IC receives the cache and maintains transfer-of-custody/accounting. MEDDRUN may also be pre-deployed for designated special events.
